summary: On the Kherson direction, a combined artillery and FPV drone strike destroyed a Ukrainian T-80U tank in Nikolaypole. Russian forces reported that Ukrainian drone strikes have caused over one trillion rubles in losses to Russian oil refineries. On the Pokrovsk direction, the 46th Brigade destroyed enemy drones, vehicles, and electronic warfare systems, with UAV operators demonstrating high effectiveness. Ukrainian forces are employing fiber-optic FPV drones with up to 50 km control range, immune to jamming, to strike deep into Russian rear areas, while also retuning control frequencies to non-standard bands. Russian logistics are reportedly collapsing under systematic Ukrainian drone attacks on supply lines and command centers. Ukrainian forces are countering Russian "Molniya" drones using log-periodic directional antennas on 3.3 GHz, which are resistant to jamming and use linear polarization. The "Elka" anti-drone system is effectively downing Ukrainian "Hornet" fixed-wing UAVs, and Russian operators are conducting daily FPV anti-drone operations, eliminating 136 targets on March 14. Ukrainian FPV pilots are using tactics to distract Russian forces before strikes, and in one instance, a Russian soldier charging at a drone with a bayonet was struck by two simultaneous FPV drones.
